label: PI-003 build — `persona` methodology entity type end-to-end (migration, access, REST, UI, tests). First of the six v0.5+ methodology-entity build sessions kicked off by SES-081's design phase (WT-049 consumed). Migration 0013 adds the personas table and extends refs/change_log CHECKs; access/repositories/persona.py + Persona model + PERSONA_STATUSES vocab + persona_scopes_to_domain / persona_realized_as_entity reference kinds + _kinds_for_pair clauses; eight REST endpoints; eight ui/client.py methods; sidebar entry; PersonasPanel + dialogs; 76 new tests. PI-003 resolves at apply time via the resolves_planning_items section.

session.summary: PI-003 (`persona` methodology entity type) shipped end-to-end. The v2 storage layer now hosts persona records — human roles and actors performed in the client's organization, surfaced in Phase 2 or Phase 3 of the evolved methodology. The build mirrors the v0.4 slice-C entity pattern verbatim with persona-specific adjustments per `persona.md` v1.0: three-status propose-verify lifecycle (candidate / confirmed / deferred) with one-way gate, case-insensitive name uniqueness, two outgoing reference kinds (`persona_scopes_to_domain` to domains; `persona_realized_as_entity` to entities — the first methodology entity to surface two outgoing kinds at the panel level), references-over-FK posture per DEC-053, soft-delete-with-restore via the standard mechanism, parent-prefix field naming, REST-via-`{data, meta, errors}`-envelope. The PersonasPanel adopts the v0.4 DEC-067 create-then-attach flow for cross-entity-type consistency. PI-003's deferred-since-DEC-039 status is resolved.

commits.commit_message_first_line: v2: PI-003 — persona methodology entity type end-to-end (migration, access, REST, UI, tests)
commits.commit_message_full:
v2: PI-003 — persona methodology entity type end-to-end (migration, access, REST, UI, tests)

Resolves PI-003 (deferred from v0.4 minimum-viable inventory per
DEC-039). Lands the `persona` methodology entity type per
`methodology-schema-specs/persona.md` v1.0 — the fifth methodology
entity type after domain / entity / process / crm_candidate.

Migration 0013 (forward + reverse):
  - Creates the `personas` table: 9 columns, 2 CHECK constraints,
    2 indexes.
  - Extends `refs.source_type` / `refs.target_type` to admit
    'persona'.
  - Extends `refs.relationship_kind` to admit
    'persona_scopes_to_domain' and 'persona_realized_as_entity'.
  - Extends `change_log.entity_type` to admit 'persona'.

Access layer:
  - `Persona` SQLAlchemy model mirroring `Entity` with persona_*
    prefix; no FK columns (references-over-FK per DEC-053).
  - `access/repositories/persona.py` — 8 module-level functions
    (list / get / next_identifier / create / update / patch /
    delete / restore) plus helpers; SAVEPOINT-retry auto-assign
    for race-free concurrent creates.
  - Vocab additions: PERSONA_STATUSES + PERSONA_STATUS_TRANSITIONS
    (three-status propose-verify with one-way gate, mirroring
    domain/entity); 'persona' in ENTITY_TYPES; two new kinds in
    REFERENCE_RELATIONSHIPS; two clauses in _kinds_for_pair.

REST API:
  - `api/routers/persona.py` — 8 endpoints with `{data, meta,
    errors}` envelope discipline; status_transition_handler
    handles dedicated invalid-transition body shape.
  - 3 Pydantic schemas (PersonaCreateIn / Replace / Patch).
  - main.py wiring.

UI:
  - 8 client methods in ui/client.py.
  - Sidebar entry 'Personas' at Methodology position #5.
  - main_window dispatch + ENTITY_TYPE_TO_SIDEBAR_LABEL entry.
  - `PersonasPanel` mirroring EntitiesPanel; first methodology
    panel to render two outgoing reference kinds in its
    ReferencesSection. Responsibilities collapsible expanded by
    default per spec §3.6.3 (client-facing content); Notes
    collapsed by default (consultant scratchpad).
  - `PersonaCreateDialog` / `PersonaEditDialog` /
    `PersonaDeleteDialog` + `_persona_schema.py` mirroring
    entity_crud.py; adopts v0.4 DEC-067 create-then-attach flow
    for cross-entity-type consistency.
  - personas.json filename routes to 'Personas' panel in the
    file-watch refresh service.
  - Exporter writes personas.json snapshots alongside the v0.4
    methodology-entity files.

Tests: 76 new tests across 3 files (38 access / 22 API / 16 UI),
covering persona.md §3.7 acceptance criteria 1–14 (criterion 12
UI dialogs end-to-end; criterion 13 vocab + _kinds_for_pair
integration through the references POST surface). Plus 4
constant-list test updates (test_exporter, test_smoke ×2,
test_crm_candidates) per the v0.4 slice-C precedent.

Test suite: 1542 → 1618 passed, 3 skipped, 0 regressions.

Authority: PI-003 (deferred per DEC-039 from v0.4 minimum-viable
inventory); persona.md v1.0 (authored under SES-081 / CONV-051 /
WT-049 as part of SES-081's design-phase orchestration of
PI-003/PI-004/PI-005). Build prompt:
CLAUDE-CODE-PROMPT-build-persona.md.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

decisions.title: `persona` identifier prefix and format
decisions.context: PI-003 build of the `persona` methodology entity type per persona.md v1.0 (§3.1). The methodology-entity-schema-spec-guide §3.1 establishes a soft-3-letter prefix posture; the v2 prefix set already includes DEC, SES, RSK, PI, TOP, REF, CHR, STA, DOM, ENT, PROC, CRM, WS, CONV, RB, WT, COP, DEP, CM, FLD, REQ, MCF, TST.
decisions.decision: Adopt `PER` as the persona identifier prefix with format `PER-NNN` (3-digit zero-padded). Server-side identifier auto-assignment on POST omission per PI-002; `GET /personas/next-identifier` helper available per DEC-043. Explicit-identifier POST validated against `^PER-\d{3}$` with collision returning 409 and malformation returning 422.
decisions.rationale: PER reads unambiguously as 'persona', has no collision with the existing v2 prefix set, and matches the soft-3-letter posture established by domain (DOM) and entity (ENT). The format and auto-assignment semantics are uniform with every other prefixed-identifier entity type in v2 (PI-002 / DEC-043 confluence).
decisions.alternatives_considered: PSN (three-letter, ambiguous between persona/person/poison). PRS (also persona-like, but reads weakly). Sticking with PERSONA-NNN (longer than the established three-letter posture; rejected for consistency).
decisions.consequences: Persona records are addressed as PER-001, PER-002, etc. The cross-entity-type identifier pattern is uniform: every prefixed methodology and governance entity in v0.5+ uses the same 3-letter + dash + 3-digit shape, and the same server-side auto-assignment helpers apply.

decisions.title: `persona` field inventory and validation under v0.5+ thin shape
decisions.context: PI-003 build per persona.md §3.2. The thin-shape posture established by entity.md is the precedent: ship the minimum-viable column set that hosts Phase 2 / early Phase 3 persona-surfacing work; grow additively as CBM-redo signal reveals what `persona` needs to carry.
decisions.decision: Persona carries five substantive fields plus inherited timestamps: `persona_name` (required, case-insensitive unique within the engagement), `persona_role_summary` (required, plain text), `persona_responsibilities` (optional, plain text), `persona_notes` (optional, plain text consultant scratchpad), `persona_status` (required enum). No `persona_purpose` field. No authority/access-boundary classification. No storage-level length caps. Parent-prefix field naming per DEC-046.
decisions.rationale: Reflects the actual Phase 2 / Phase 3 surfacing pattern from the evolved methodology's research: role name + role summary captures the surfacing reality without inventing a separate persona_purpose artifact (the role summary already explains what the role does). Optional responsibilities and notes accommodate the cases where the consultant decomposes the summary or captures pattern-library rationale. Authority/access-boundary classification belongs to a later release once the methodology surfaces the required granularity and once integration with CRM-engine role models is understood.
decisions.alternatives_considered: Include `persona_purpose` parallel to `domain_purpose` (rejected — would duplicate role_summary or invent methodology content). Include `persona_authority` with a 3-or-4-value enum (rejected — premature; CRM-engine coupling unsettled). Add storage-level length caps to role_summary / responsibilities (rejected — easy to add via migration if CBM-redo surfaces pathological-input need).
decisions.consequences: The personas table is small (9 columns). The schema is additive; growth happens via Alembic migrations adding columns when CBM-redo signal motivates them. Three v0.6+ PIs are anticipated for the deferred classification work (persona authority / membership cardinality / realization mechanism reconsideration) per persona.md §3.8.3.

decisions.title: `persona` status lifecycle: three-status propose-verify with one-way gate
decisions.context: PI-003 build per persona.md §3.4. The domain (DEC-047) and entity (DEC-052) precedents established the three-status propose-verify lifecycle as the pattern for methodology entities surfaced by the consultant and verified by the client.
decisions.decision: Persona adopts the three-status propose-verify lifecycle exactly: candidate (default starter) → confirmed or deferred (one-way gate, no regression to candidate); confirmed ↔ deferred allowed in both directions to support mid-engagement scope changes. No archived status — soft-delete covers the retained-for-record use case. Rejection of a candidate is handled by soft-delete, not by a status value. `persona_status` is independent of any affiliated domain's status and of the realization entity's status (lifecycles managed separately).
decisions.rationale: Cross-entity-type consistency: personas, like domains and entities, are surfaced by the consultant (typically as candidates), verified by the client (transition to confirmed or deferred), and may be rejected entirely (soft-delete). The independence-from-affiliation-status rule keeps lifecycles tractable when a persona scopes to multiple domains at different lifecycle positions and realizes as an entity at a different position.
decisions.alternatives_considered: Add a separate `rejected` status (rejected — duplicates soft-delete). Add an `archived` status (rejected — soft-delete + show-deleted toggle already covers this). Cascade status from affiliated domains (rejected — couples lifecycles that should be independent; persona Program Manager is confirmed regardless of which specific domains it ends up in).
decisions.consequences: The persona-status code path is byte-for-byte identical with domain and entity. The access layer's `_check_transition` consults only the persona's own status. Status changes never query the refs table or the affiliated/realization records. UI status combo restricts to valid successors via the schema's `compute_options` callback.

decisions.title: `persona`-to-`domain` affiliation and `persona`-to-`entity` realization mechanisms; new vocab kinds registered
decisions.context: PI-003 build per persona.md §3.3.1. The DEC-053 references-over-FK precedent established that many-to-many methodology affiliations live in the universal references table rather than as FK columns on the source-entity table.
decisions.decision: Both `persona_scopes_to_domain` (persona → domain, many-to-many) and `persona_realized_as_entity` (persona → entity, conceptually optional and most often single-target but mechanism permits multi-target) are implemented via the references entity at v2's `refs` table. Two new vocabulary entries registered in `REFERENCE_RELATIONSHIPS`; two new clauses added to `_kinds_for_pair` enforcing the (persona, domain) and (persona, entity) source/target constraints; Alembic migration 0013 extends the `refs.relationship_kind` CHECK constraint to include both new values. Zero-affiliation and zero-realization explicitly permitted. Duplicate `(source_id, target_id, relationship_kind)` tuples rejected by the references-table uniqueness constraint.
decisions.rationale: Personas legitimately scope to multiple domains (a Program Manager spans MN/MR/FU at CBM); a single-value FK would break that. Personas mostly realize as at most one entity, but the references mechanism permits the few that need multi-realization without validation overhead and without forcing a schema change later. Cross-entity-type consistency with `entity_scopes_to_domain` (DEC-053) keeps the audit/reverse-query story uniform.
decisions.alternatives_considered: Single-value FK `persona_realized_as_entity_identifier` column on personas (rejected — locks out the multi-realization case and creates a schema-shape inconsistency between affiliations and realization). Inline-affiliation field on persona create/update bodies (rejected — would break the v2 decomposed-references discipline DEC-006 establishes). Force the realization to be single-target via access-layer validation (rejected — premature; if CBM-redo surfaces friction with multi-target, PI-XXX-C / persona.md §3.8.3 records the reconsideration option).
decisions.consequences: The PersonasPanel is the first methodology panel to surface two outgoing reference kinds in its ReferencesSection. The references-create cascading dialog already drives its filtering from RELATIONSHIP_RULES so no UI changes were needed for the dialog itself — the two new kinds appear automatically once the vocab and `_kinds_for_pair` clauses land. Inverse queries are trivial: `GET /references?target_type=domain&target_id=DOM-NNN&relationship_kind=persona_scopes_to_domain` returns all personas scoped to that domain, and similarly for entity realization.

decisions.title: `persona` API surface, UI defaults, and create-dialog flow choice for v0.5+
decisions.context: PI-003 build per persona.md §3.5–3.6. The spec left the create-dialog domain-affiliation and entity-realization flow as an open question for the v0.5+ build to settle (§3.8.1); both create-then-attach and create-with-attach are spec-equivalent.
decisions.decision: Adopt the v0.4 DEC-067 create-then-attach flow unchanged for the PersonaCreateDialog: the dialog creates the persona record only, with no domain-affiliation multi-select and no entity-realization single-select; the user attaches `persona_scopes_to_domain` and `persona_realized_as_entity` references from the detail pane's ReferencesSection 'Add reference' affordance after the persona record exists. Standard endpoint set with no deviations (eight endpoints from persona.md §3.5.1); decomposed reference handling (no shortcut endpoints for affiliation or realization); default ListDetailPanel UI under the existing Methodology sidebar group at position #5; four master-pane columns (Identifier / Name / Status / Updated) per spec §3.6.2; detail pane renders fields in §3.2 order with Responsibilities collapsible expanded-by-default (client-facing content) and Notes collapsed-by-default (consultant scratchpad); 14 testable acceptance criteria per spec §3.7.
decisions.rationale: Cross-entity-type consistency is the primary driver: the v0.4 entity build (slice C, DEC-067) adopted create-then-attach and users have already internalized the affordance. Forcing two different flows for two adjacent methodology entity types creates cognitive overhead with no clear payoff. The decomposed reference handling matches the v2 references-first discipline (DEC-006) and the standard endpoint set keeps the API surface uniform with the seven prior methodology and governance entity types.
decisions.alternatives_considered: Create-with-attach flow with multi-select for domains and single-select for entity realization (rejected for the consistency rationale above; the spec recorded both as equivalent and the v0.4 build chose create-then-attach). Shortcut endpoints `/personas/{id}/scopes` and `/personas/{id}/realizes` (rejected — would break decomposed-references discipline). Adding inline-affiliation fields to the create body (rejected — same reason).
decisions.consequences: PersonaCreateDialog matches EntityCreateDialog line-by-line in structure. The user gesture to author a persona with domain affiliations and entity realization is two-step (create record, then add references from the detail pane), matching the existing v0.4 entity pattern exactly. The ReferencesSection 'Add reference' affordance is the single UI control surfacing both reference kinds (the cascading dialog filters by source/target type and offers the relationship kinds from `_kinds_for_pair`).
